cases.name: pydantic_ai_python_support_retained_value
cases.answer: The same guide still documents ThinkingPart support from the earlier v0.3.0 release notes. Pydantic AI v1.0.0 dropped support for Python 3.9, so Python 3.10+ remained supported.

cases.name: fastapi_python_ceiling_updated_plus_retained
cases.answer: FastAPI 0.119.0 added support for Python 3.14, while Python 3.13 still remained supported in the same later release-note stream. FastAPI higher-end Python support after the next upgrade.

cases.name: fastapi_python_support_updated_plus_retained
cases.answer: The official FastAPI release notes updated internal types for Python 3.10 and migrated docs from Python 3.9 to Python 3.10. FastAPI 0.129.0 dropped support for Python 3.9, so Python 3.10+ remained supported.

cases.name: pytest_python_support_updated_plus_retained
cases.answer: pytest dropped support for Python 3.8, so Python 3.9+ remained supported. Python 3.10 remains supported, and the changelog still records Python 3.10 fixes.

cases.name: pytest_python_ceiling_updated_plus_retained
cases.answer: pytest 9.0.0 added official support for Python 3.14, while Python 3.13 still remained supported in the same later changelog stream. pytest higher-end Python support after the next upgrade.

cases.name: fastapi_pydantic_migration_updated_plus_retained
cases.answer: Since FastAPI 0.119.0, there is also partial support for Pydantic v1 from inside of Pydantic v2, to facilitate the migration to v2. Pydantic v2 still remains the supported migration target, and FastAPI later ran performance tests only on Pydantic v2.

cases.name: typer_python_support_updated_plus_retained
cases.answer: Typer later dropped support for Python 3.9, so Python 3.10+ remained supported. The same release stream still targets Python 3.10 in its types and docs migration notes.

cases.name: httpx_app_shortcut_updated_plus_retained
cases.answer: The explicit transport style with `transport=httpx.WSGITransport()` or `transport=httpx.ASGITransport()` remained the documented path after the removal. In HTTPX 0.28.0, the `app` argument has now been removed.

cases.name: httpx_proxy_cleanup_updated_plus_retained
cases.answer: The same official release stream still points users to the replacement proxy argument, or mounts for more complex configurations. HTTPX 0.28.0 says the deprecated proxies argument has now been removed.

cases.name: uvicorn_python_support_updated_plus_retained
cases.answer: Uvicorn later dropped Python 3.9, so Python 3.10+ remained supported. The same official release stream still includes support for Python 3.14.

cases.name: pydantic_ai_python_support_updated_plus_retained
cases.answer: Pydantic AI v1.0.0 dropped support for Python 3.9, so Python 3.10+ remained supported. The same guide still documents ThinkingPart support from the earlier v0.3.0 release notes.

cases.name: fastapi_pydantic_migration_conflict_synthesis_1
cases.answer: Since FastAPI 0.119.0, there is also partial support for Pydantic v1 from inside of Pydantic v2, to facilitate the migration to v2. Pydantic v2 still remains the supported migration target, and FastAPI later ran performance tests only on Pydantic v2.

cases.name: fastapi_pydantic_migration_conflict_synthesis_2
cases.answer: Using pydantic.v1 is not supported in Python 3.14 and above. Since FastAPI 0.119.0, there is also partial support for Pydantic v1 from inside of Pydantic v2, to facilitate the migration to v2. FastAPI later dropped support for Pydantic v1, first keeping only short temporary support for Pydantic v2's pydantic.v1 and then dropping pydantic.v1 support outright.
